title: Dicraeia elongata
intro: Dicraeia elongata, commonly known as the "Coralroot," is a rare and unique plant species belonging to the family Corallorhizaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dicraeia elongata include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dicraeia elongata include teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is largely restricted to traditional medicine and is not widely available in commercial markets.
taxonomy: Dicraeia elongata is a species of coral-like plant that belongs to the family Zosterophyllaceae. It is classified within the division Tracheophyta, which includes vascular plants. The class of Dicraeia elongata is Polypodiopsida, a group of vascular plants that includes ferns and their allies. The order of this plant is Zosterophyllales, a small group of extinct vascular plants. Dicraeia elongata is considered to be an extinct genus.
